concept.text: Did you know? A baby kangaroo is called a joey! Giraffes have very long necks!

questions.prompt: A baby kangaroo is called a...
How many arms does an octopus have?

concept.text: Bananas are yellow! Strawberries are red! Foods come in lots of fun colors!
concept.funFact: A pumpkin is actually a fruit, not a vegetable!

concept.text: Clouds float in the sky! Birds fly! Sometimes airplanes too!
concept.funFact: Birds use their wings to fly!

concept.text: Toys are fun! A ball bounces. Blocks stack up. Crayons make colorful drawings!
concept.funFact: The first crayons were made over 100 years ago!

concept.text: There are MANY stars in the sky at night! More than we can count!
concept.funFact: The sun is a star too!

concept.text: An octopus has 3 hearts! Sharks have been around longer than trees! A cat's purr can help heal bones! The natural world is full of incredible surprises.
concept.funFact: Honey never spoils — ancient Egyptian honey is still edible today!

questions.prompt: How many hearts does an octopus have?

concept.text: The cheetah is the fastest land animal at 70 mph. Mount Everest is the tallest mountain. The blue whale is the largest animal ever. The Nile is the longest river.
concept.funFact: The blue whale's heart is as big as a small car!

questions.prompt: Tallest mountain on Earth is Mount ____.
How fast can a cheetah run?

concept.text: The Olympics happen every 4 years. Soccer (football) is the world's most popular sport. A marathon is 26.2 miles long. Basketball was invented in 1891 in the USA.
concept.funFact: Michael Phelps has won 23 Olympic gold medals — the most ever!

concept.text: Harry Potter was written by J.K. Rowling. Disney's Mickey Mouse was created in 1928. The Lord of the Rings was written by J.R.R. Tolkien. Stories spark our imagination!
concept.funFact: Harry Potter has been translated into over 80 languages!

questions.prompt: Mickey Mouse was created by ____ Disney.
Who wrote Lord of the Rings?

concept.text: Time for a mixed-up round! Quick questions about science, history, geography — let's see what you remember!
concept.funFact: Mixing up topics actually helps your brain learn better!

concept.text: A teaspoonful of neutron star material would weigh 6 billion tons on Earth. Bananas are slightly radioactive (from potassium). Octopuses have blue blood (due to copper).
concept.funFact: Lightning is about 5x hotter than the surface of the Sun!

questions.prompt: Why do octopuses have blue blood?
Lightning is hotter than the Sun's surface.
Bananas are slightly radioactive because of ____.

concept.text: The first computer mouse was made of wood in 1964 by Douglas Engelbart. The internet's predecessor, ARPANET, sent its first message in 1969. The first email was sent in 1971.
concept.funFact: Over 90% of the world's data was created in just the last few years!

questions.prompt: When was the first computer mouse made?
The first computer mouse was made of wood.
The first email was sent in...

concept.text: Mandarin Chinese has the most native speakers. The world's smallest country is Vatican City. India has 22 official languages. Bhutan measures national happiness (GNH).
concept.funFact: There are around 7,000 languages spoken in the world today!

questions.prompt: The world's smallest country is ____ City.
About how many languages exist worldwide?

concept.text: The Great Wall of China was built over many dynasties starting around 7th century BCE. The Mona Lisa was painted around 1503–1506. The first moon landing was 1969 (Apollo 11).
concept.funFact: Cleopatra lived closer to the iPhone's invention than to the building of the pyramids!

questions.prompt: Year of the first moon landing?
Mission name of the first moon landing: ____ 11.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to today than to the pyramids being built.
The Mona Lisa was painted in...

concept.text: The Champion final mix: history, geography, math, music, and more all in one round. Take a breath — and bring it home!
concept.funFact: Reviewing across topics is one of the strongest ways to lock in learning!

questions.prompt: The Pacific Ring of ____ has many volcanoes.
The major scale has 7 different notes.
Order events: WWI ends, Moon landing, Internet invented (approx).
